sets.q: A 15 N force is applied perpendicular to a lever 0.5 m from the pivot. What is the torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d
Force is perpendicular — no angle needed.

sets.q: A 20 N force is applied perpendicular to a lever 0.4 m from the pivot. What is the torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d
Force is perpendicular — no angle needed.

sets.q: A 10 N force is applied perpendicular to a lever 0.6 m from the pivot. What is the torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d
Force is perpendicular — no angle needed.

sets.q: A 25 N force is applied perpendicular to a lever 0.3 m from the pivot. What is the torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d
Force is perpendicular — no angle needed.

sets.q: A 12 N force is applied perpendicular to a lever 0.75 m from the pivot. What is the torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d
Force is perpendicular — no angle needed.

sets.q: What is the unit of torque?
sets.given:
Torque = Force × distance.
Force is in Newtons (N); distance is in meters (m).

sets.q: Which of the following is the correct SI unit for torque?
sets.given: Torque = Force × distance.

sets.q: Torque is measured in which unit?
sets.given: Torque = Force × distance = N × m.

sets.q: Which unit correctly expresses torque in the SI system?
sets.given: Torque = Force × perpendicular distance.

sets.q: The SI unit of torque is equivalent to which combination?
sets.given: Torque = F × d. Force unit = N, distance unit = m.

sets.q: A force of 30 N is applied at 90° to a lever arm of 0.6 m. What is the torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d × sin θ
sin 90° = 1

sets.q: A force of 40 N is applied at 90° to a lever arm of 0.5 m. What is the torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d × sin θ
sin 90° = 1

sets.q: A force of 50 N is applied at 90° to a lever arm of 0.8 m. What is the torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d × sin θ
sin 90° = 1

sets.q: A force of 25 N is applied at 90° to a lever arm of 1.2 m. What is the torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d × sin θ
sin 90° = 1

sets.q: A force of 60 N is applied at 90° to a lever arm of 0.4 m. What is the torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d × sin θ
sin 90° = 1

sets.q: A 60 N force is applied at 0° to a lever arm (parallel to it). What is the torque produced?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d × sin θ
sin 0° = 0

sets.q: A 45 N force is applied parallel to a lever arm. What is the resulting torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d × sin θ
Parallel means θ = 0°, sin 0° = 0

sets.q: A force acts along the same direction as a 2 m lever arm. What torque does it produce?
sets.given:
A force parallel to the lever arm has θ = 0°
τ = F × d × sin 0° = 0

sets.q: If a force of 80 N acts at 0° to a wrench handle, what torque is produced?
sets.given: sin 0° = 0, so τ = F × d × 0 = 0

sets.q: A 100 N force is applied exactly along the length of a lever arm. What torque results?
sets.given: τ = F × d × sin θ. Force along lever arm means θ = 0°.

sets.q: At what angle should a force be applied to a lever arm to produce maximum torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d × sin θ
Consider which angle maximizes sin θ.

sets.q: For a given force and lever arm length, torque is greatest when the angle between them is:
sets.given: τ = F × d × sin θ — find the θ that maximizes this expression.

sets.q: Which angle between a force and its lever arm produces the maximum torque?
sets.given: Maximum value of sin θ occurs at θ = ?

sets.q: A mechanic wants to apply the most torque possible to a bolt. At what angle to the wrench handle should the force be applied?
sets.given: τ = F × d × sin θ is maximized when sin θ is maximum.

sets.q: To maximize the torque on a nut, a force should be applied at what angle to the wrench?
sets.given: sin θ has a maximum value of 1 at θ = 90°.

sets.q: A torque of 24 N·m is produced by a perpendicular force applied 0.4 m from the pivot. What is the force?
sets.given:
Rearrange: τ = F × d
→ F = τ / d

sets.q: A torque of 36 N·m is produced by a perpendicular force applied 0.6 m from the pivot. What is the force?
sets.given: Rearrange: τ = F × d → F = τ / d

sets.q: A torque of 48 N·m is produced by a perpendicular force applied 0.8 m from the pivot. What is the force?
sets.given: Rearrange: τ = F × d → F = τ / d

sets.q: A perpendicular force applied 0.5 m from a pivot produces 30 N·m of torque. What is the magnitude of the force?
sets.given: F = τ / d

sets.q: A torque of 18 N·m is produced by a perpendicular force applied 0.3 m from the pivot. What is the force?
sets.given: F = τ / d

sets.q: A 100 N weight sits 3 m from the pivot on one side of a seesaw. What force must be applied 5 m from the pivot on the other side to balance it?
sets.given:
Rotational equilibrium: F₁ × d₁ = F₂ × d₂
Solve for F₂.

sets.q: A 90 N weight sits 4 m from the pivot. What force at 6 m balances the seesaw?
sets.given: F₁ × d₁ = F₂ × d₂

sets.q: A 120 N weight is placed 2 m from a pivot. What force at 4 m from the pivot on the other side maintains equilibrium?
sets.given: F₁ × d₁ = F₂ × d₂

sets.q: A 80 N force acts 3 m from a pivot. What force at 4 m on the opposite side creates rotational equilibrium?
sets.given: Rotational equilibrium: F₁d₁ = F₂d₂

sets.q: A 150 N load hangs 2 m from a pivot. What upward force at 5 m from the pivot keeps the beam level?
sets.given: F₁ × d₁ = F₂ × d₂

sets.q: Which of the following changes would NOT increase the torque on a bolt being turned by a wrench?
sets.given:
τ = F × d × sin θ
Consider each factor that affects torque.
opts: Applying the force closer to the pivot
Applying the force at 90° to the wrench

sets.q: A mechanic wants to increase torque on a stubborn nut. Which action would NOT help?
sets.given: τ = F × d × sin θ — consider what each factor does.
opts: Gripping the wrench near the bolt head
Using a longer extension on the wrench

sets.q: Which action reduces the torque applied to a bolt?
sets.given: τ = F × d × sin θ

sets.q: Which of the following would decrease torque when turning a bolt with a wrench?
sets.given: τ = F × d × sin θ — identify what decreases torque.
opts: Moving your grip closer to the bolt

sets.q: To reduce torque when tightening a screw, which change should you make?
sets.given: τ = F × d × sin θ — what reduces the result?
opts: Apply force closer to the pivot point
Apply force perpendicular to the handle

sets.q: A force of 40 N is applied at 30° to a 1.5 m lever arm. What is the torque?
sets.given:
Formula: τ = F × d × sin θ
sin 30° = 0.5

sets.q: A force of 50 N is applied at 30° to a 1.2 m lever arm. What is the torque?
sets.given: τ = F × d × sin θ, sin 30° = 0.5

sets.q: A force of 60 N is applied at 30° to a 1.0 m lever arm. What is the torque?
sets.given: τ = F × d × sin θ, sin 30° = 0.5

sets.q: A force of 80 N is applied at 30° to a 0.75 m lever arm. What is the torque?
sets.given: τ = F × d × sin θ, sin 30° = 0.5

sets.q: A force of 120 N is applied at 30° to a 0.5 m lever arm. What is the torque?
sets.given: τ = F × d × sin θ, sin 30° = 0.5

sets.q: Two forces act on opposite sides of a pivot. Force A = 25 N at 2 m. Force B = 50 N at 1 m. Is the system in rotational equilibrium?
sets.given:
Equilibrium requires: τ_A = τ_B
Compare torques on each side.

sets.q: Force A = 30 N acts 3 m from a pivot. Force B = 45 N acts 2 m from the same pivot on the opposite side. Is the beam balanced?
sets.given: Calculate τ_A and τ_B. If equal, the system is in rotational equilibrium.

sets.q: A 40 N force at 5 m and a 100 N force at 2 m act on opposite sides of a fulcrum. Is the system balanced?
sets.given: τ = F × d. Compare torques on each side.
opts: No — the 40 N side has more torque
No — the 100 N side has more torque

sets.q: A beam has 60 N at 4 m on the left and 80 N at 3 m on the right. Is it in equilibrium?
sets.given: τ_left = 60 × 4, τ_right = 80 × 3

sets.q: Force X = 35 N at 6 m left of pivot. Force Y = 70 N at 3 m right of pivot. Is the system in rotational equilibrium?
